step_by_step_thinking:
Impression compound bases typically exhibit high fusion temperatures due to their crystalline nature and ability to form strong intermolecular bonds upon cooling after being heated during fabrication. Therefore, option D "High fusion temperature" aligns best with the properties expected of impression compound bases.
\boxed{D} Based on the description of impression compound bases' physical properties, particularly their need to maintain stability and shape post-fabrication through a process involving heat-induced changes, the characteristic of having a high fusion temperature fits well with maintaining structural integrity when cooled down following processing.
